title: Sugar restriction in the first 1000 days after conception, and long-term respiratory health: a quasi-experiment study.
ehr_evidence: Outcomes included incident asthma, COPD, and IPF (ascertained by health records)
summary: A quasi-experimental study investigating the long-term effects of early-life sugar restriction (based on UK sugar rationing) on adult respiratory health, including asthma, COPD, and IPF, and lung function.
key_findings: Early-life sugar rationing associated with lower risks of asthma (HR 0.75) and COPD (HR 0.73)
Rationed participants had higher mean FEV1%, FVC%, and FEV1/FVC ratio
Diabetes and hypertension mediated 18.2% of the effect
data_availability_statement: The data are accessible through a public, open-access repository . Data from the UKB, ELSA, and HRS are available to researchers on request.
